title: Buttery Juicy Mexican Black Pudding Frijoles Charros
description: A Mexican-inspired twist on the classic Frijoles Charros recipe featuring black pudding as the star ingredient. This dish is sure to satisfy your cravings on a sick day with its rich, buttery flavors and hearty, high-protein ingredients.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, brown the ground beef over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spatula as it cooks.
Once browned, add the diced onion and minced garlic and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the cubed black pudding and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the diced tomatoes, red kidney beans, cumin, smoked paprika, chili powder, salt, and ground black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and let cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the unsalted butter until melted, then taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro.
Adjust serving size as needed based on individual appetites.
Discard any leftovers or refrigerate for up to 3 days.
Reheat in the oven or on the stovetop before serving.
